name: Jack Daniel's Old No. 7
notes: banana, caramel, vanilla, toasted oak, light smoke, brown sugar
research: Among most-purchased American spirits nationwide in 2025 (Royal Batch).
best: Jack Daniel's name ask, Tennessee whiskey curious, classic bar call.
avoid: User wants Kentucky bourbon or Scotch only.
why: Recommend when the member wants the iconic Jack pour — banana bread sweetness and charcoal-mellowed Tennessee oak.
catalogLine: Jack Daniel's Old No. 7 · Tennessee · $28 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Journey: Novice · Notes: banana, caramel
blurb: Jack Daniel's Old No. 7 — the black-label Tennessee whiskey America never stopped buying. Banana, caramel, and charcoal mellow beside a Habano.

name: Jack Daniel's Single Barrel Select
best: Jack Daniel's request, Tennessee whiskey curious, Habano pairing.
avoid: User wants Kentucky bourbon or Scotch only.
why: Recommend when the member wants Tennessee character — banana bread, caramel, and toasted oak.
catalogLine: Jack Daniel's Single Barrel Select · Tennessee · $55 · 94 proof (47.0% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: banana, caramel
blurb: Jack Daniel's Single Barrel — banana and caramel through charcoal-mellowed Tennessee oak. The Jack pour worth recommending beside a cigar.

name: Jefferson's Ocean Aged at Sea
notes: sea salt, brine, caramel, sweetness, vanilla, oak
best: Demo night, gift story, member wants something to discuss.
why: Recommend when a story pour matters — caramel and brine from ocean-aged bourbon beside a cigar.
catalogLine: Jefferson's Ocean Aged at Sea · Kentucky · $85 · 90 proof (45.0% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: caramel, vanilla
blurb: Jefferson's Ocean — bourbon that crossed the sea. Brine and caramel with a story every lounge guest asks about.

name: Jim Beam Kentucky Straight Bourbon
notes: vanilla, caramel, oak, light corn sweetness, gentle spice, toasted nuts
research: Household bourbon staple in 2025 American whiskey sales (Royal Batch).
best: Bourbon beginner, Old Fashioned base, budget Kentucky pour.
avoid: User wants allocated or high-proof only.
why: Recommend as America's everyday bourbon — vanilla, caramel, and oak without pretense.
catalogLine: Jim Beam Kentucky Straight Bourbon · Kentucky · $20 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Journey: Novice · Notes: vanilla, caramel
blurb: Jim Beam White Label — the bourbon in every American pantry. Vanilla and caramel Kentucky honesty beside a medium cigar.

name: Johnnie Walker Black Label
notes: dark fruit, sweet vanilla, creamy toffee, smooth smoke
best: Gift pour, blend curious, won't spend Blue money.
why: Recommend when prestige Scotch is needed at accessible price — honey, smoke, dried fruit.
catalogLine: Johnnie Walker Black Label · Scotland · $42 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: honey, smoke
blurb: Johnnie Walker Black — honey and gentle smoke in the blend everyone knows. Prestige without the Blue Label receipt.

name: Johnnie Walker Blue Label
notes: smoke, raisins, dried fruit, citrus, honey, sweet spice, vanilla, chocolate
best: Gift pour, status request, member names Blue Label, lounge demo prestige.
avoid: User wants single-malt purist only or budget under $50.
why: Recommend when prestige recognition matters — honeyed blend with smoke and dark fruit, universally known bottle.
catalogLine: Johnnie Walker Blue Label · Scotland · $225 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: honey, dark fruit
blurb: Johnnie Walker Blue — instant prestige in the glass. Honey, dark fruit, and a whisper of smoke; value debated, recognition undeniable.

name: Johnnie Walker Green Label
notes: garden fruit, floral, oak, cedar, peat smoke
best: Malt blend fan, medium-full cigar, discontinued-era prestige still stocked.
why: Recommend when the member wants vatted malt character — smoke, cedar, and citrus without grain spirit.
catalogLine: Johnnie Walker Green Label · Scotland · $65 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: smoke, cedar
blurb: Johnnie Walker Green — blended malts only, with smoke and cedar. The vatted malt pour for members between Black and Blue.

name: Ketel One Vodka
notes: citrus, honey, crisp, smooth, long finish
best: Vodka soda, martini base, member wants neutral spirit.
why: Recommend when the member wants a clean neutral spirit — citrus peel and mineral.
catalogLine: Ketel One Vodka · Netherlands · $28 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: clean, citrus peel
blurb: Ketel One — crisp Dutch vodka with citrus peel and mineral. Clean enough to let a flavored Connecticut cigar speak.

name: Knob Creek Kentucky Straight Bourbon
notes: toasted nuts, grain, oak, vanilla, caramel, fruity
best: Member wants age and proof without Booker's extremes.
why: Recommend when 9-year oak and 100 proof should stand up to a robusto — peanut, caramel, leather.
catalogLine: Knob Creek Kentucky Straight Bourbon · Kentucky · $40 · 100 proof (50.0% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: peanut, vanilla
blurb: Knob Creek 9 Year — peanut and oak at 100 proof. Age and strength in a pour that respects a robusto without going full cask strength.

name: Lagavulin 16 Year
notes: peat smoke, iodine, seaweed, sweetness, sea salt, wood
best: Member asks for smoky Scotch, peat, or bold cigar needs maritime smoke.
avoid: Peat-averse beginner or user wants light Speyside only.
why: Recommend when the member wants to understand peat — campfire, sea salt, and smoke done with balance.
catalogLine: Lagavulin 16 Year · Islay, Scotland · $110 · 86 proof (43% ABV) · Notes: campfire, sea salt
blurb: Lagavulin 16 — campfire, sea salt, and Islay smoke in a legendary 16-year pour. The Scotch that teaches peat without apology.
imageUrl: The best pairings either heavily double down on earthy/peaty notes or provide a contrasting sweetness (like chocolate or caramel) to neutralize the salty tang of the Scotch. raisin, almond, and butterscotch sweetness. This introduces a contrasting sweet/nutty note that cuts the briny, seared-wood notes of the whisky.
sterlon: Padrón Serie 1926 Maduro, My Father No. 3 Crema,  Ashton VSG (Virgin Sun Grown), Romeo y Julieta 1875 Deluxe No. 1, Partagas 898

name: Lagavulin 8 Year
notes: milk chocolate, lemon, tea smoke, barley malt, peat, pepper, dark chocolate, mint
best: Peat lover, wants intensity below 16 price, bold cigar.
why: Recommend when Lagavulin character is right but the member wants more punch — pepper, peat, 96 proof.
catalogLine: Lagavulin 8 Year · Islay, Scotland · $75 · 96 proof (48.0% ABV) · Notes: peat, smoke
blurb: Lagavulin 8 — younger Islay at 96 proof. Peat and pepper for nights when the 16-year feels too gentle.

name: Laphroaig 10 Year
notes: peat smoke, seaweed, sweetness, salt, medicinal, vanilla
best: Member asks how smoky, peat comparison, bold cigar.
why: Recommend when the member wants to understand Islay intensity — iodine, peat, and sea beside Lagavulin.
catalogLine: Laphroaig 10 Year · Islay, Scotland · $55 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: iodine, peat smoke
blurb: Laphroaig 10 — medicinal peat and iodine on Islay. The smoke lesson for members who found Lagavulin almost too polite.

name: Lunazul Blanco Tequila
notes: cooked agave, citrus, white pepper, mineral, light herbal, clean finish
research: Strong accessible tequila performer in 2025 US sales (Royal Batch).
best: Tequila beginner, margarita night, authentic agave without premium price.
avoid: User wants only luxury añejo sweetness.
why: Recommend when the member wants real blanco agave on a budget — pepper, citrus, and cooked agave.
catalogLine: Lunazul Blanco Tequila · Jalisco, Mexico · $22 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: cooked agave, citrus
blurb: Lunazul Blanco — 100% agave authenticity without the premium sticker. The tequila Americans bought when they were not splurging on Patrón.

name: The Macallan 12 Year Sherry Oak
notes: vanilla, dried fruit, oak, spice, wood smoke, sherry sweetness
best: Member wants recognizable luxury Scotch, sherry sweetness, special occasion.
avoid: User wants peat smoke or lowest price Scotch.
why: Recommend when luxury Scotch perception matters — sherry, dried fruit, and chocolate beside a premium cigar.
catalogLine: The Macallan 12 Year Sherry Oak · Speyside, Scotland · $70 · 86 proof (43% ABV) · Notes: sherry, dried fruit
blurb: Macallan 12 Sherry Oak — the luxury Scotch name everyone recognizes. Dried fruit, chocolate, and sherried depth without peat.
